question_title: Will the community prediction be higher than 43.00% on 2026-04-09 for the Metaculus question "Will OpenAI file for an IPO during 2026?"?
summary: This meta-prediction asks whether the Metaculus CP for "Will OpenAI file for an IPO during 2026?" will be strictly above 43% on April 9, 2026. The CP is currently exactly at 43% (as of March 29), meaning the status quo resolves NO. Historical CP has oscillated between 38-44% with a mean of ~41.3% and strong negative autocorrelation (-0.85), suggesting mean-reversion pressure pulling CP downward. However, the SoftBank $40B loan announcement (March 27) and continued IPO preparation media coverage are genuine bullish catalysts that could sustain or push CP higher. Monte Carlo simulation across weighted scenarios yields P(CP > 43) ≈ 42%. The knife-edge situation with CP exactly at threshold and the strictly-higher requirement slightly favors NO, with the net balance of mean reversion vs. news catalysts landing around 43%.

factors.description: CP exactly at threshold (43%); strictly higher requirement means status quo resolves NO
Strong historical mean reversion pattern (autocorrelation -0.85, mean ~41.3%); CP at 43 is above equilibrium suggesting downward pull
Alternating up-down weekly pattern; last move was +3 (up), pattern suggests next move could be down
SoftBank $40B unsecured loan (March 27) is a genuine new bullish signal for OpenAI IPO, could sustain or push CP higher
April 1 SoftBank second $10B tranche expected; could serve as additional catalyst in the resolution window
Major media coverage (CNBC, TechCrunch, Morningstar) discussing OpenAI IPO preparation increases forecaster attention and could push CP up
No S-1 filing imminent (expected Q3 2026); absence of concrete filing action limits upward movement
MC simulation weighted average gives P(CP>43) ≈ 0.42, empirical frequency of CP>43 is only 1/6 (17%)

reasoning:
**Forecast: 0.43 (43%)**

Key reasoning:

- **Status quo favors NO**: CP is exactly at the 43% threshold, and "strictly higher" means the default outcome if nothing changes is NO.
- **Mean reversion pressure**: Historical CP averages ~41.3% with strong negative autocorrelation (-0.85). At 43%, CP is above its equilibrium, suggesting downward pull.
- **Bullish catalysts partially offset**: SoftBank's $40B loan (March 27) and extensive IPO media coverage are genuine upward signals, but they appear already reflected in the 40→43 jump observed in the March 22-29 window.
- **Monte Carlo simulation**: Weighted across three scenarios (pure mean reversion, news-adjusted, equilibrium shift), the model yields P(CP > 43) ≈ 42%.
- **Upcoming potential catalyst**: SoftBank's second $10B tranche on April 1 could provide a modest additional push, but no S-1 filing is expected before Q3 2026.

The situation is genuinely close to a coin flip but tilts slightly toward NO given the mean-reversion dynamics and the strictly-higher requirement.

resolution_criteria: This question will resolve based on the community prediction of the Metaculus question found [here](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/41141) (i.e. the 'target question'). If the community prediction of the target question on 2026-04-09 23:14:54 is higher than 43.00%, this question will resolve to 'Yes'. If it is lower or equal to 43.00%, this question will resolve to 'No'. If the target question has already resolved before this question opens, then this question will be annulled. If the target question resolves after this question opens, but before 2026-04-09 23:14:54, then this question will resolve to the same value as the target question.
